title: Privacy-safe profiles, richer mentions, and restored server management controls

requirements.text: Remove any public display of user Principal IDs across the entire UI (including public profiles, profile popovers, post/message author areas, server lists, DM lists, and any other user surfaces). The app must never show another user’s Principal ID anywhere in the UI.

quotes: NEVER show the users principal id AT ALL, unless if the user goes into their profile and manually shows it with a button.
please remove the principal id being SHOWN anywhere, except on like a hide/show toggle on the users profile (NOT public profile, like profile settings and only the user can see)

acceptanceCriteria: When viewing any other user, no UI surface renders their Principal ID.
No lists (feed, servers, channels, DMs, search/autocomplete results, popovers) show Principal IDs.

requirements.text: In the signed-in user’s Profile & Settings page, add a user-only control to reveal/hide their own Principal ID (hidden by default on load), and include a copy-to-clipboard action. Do not show the Principal ID on this page unless the user explicitly chooses to reveal it.

quotes: unless if the user goes into their profile and manually shows it with a button

acceptanceCriteria: On initial load of Profile/Settings, the Principal ID is not visible.
Clicking “Show my ID” reveals the Principal ID; clicking “Hide my ID” hides it again.
A “Copy” action copies the Principal ID to the clipboard and provides a visible success confirmation.

requirements.text: Extend backend user profiles to support additional fields (at minimum: bio and join date) and per-field public/private visibility controls for all profile fields (including display name, avatar, bio, join date, and any other stored profile fields). Public profile queries must only return fields marked public to non-owners, while the profile owner must be able to fetch and edit all fields and visibility settings.

quotes: lets kinda do all of those.
lets make those added too, maybe let the user decide also

acceptanceCriteria: Profile storage includes bio and join date fields.
Profile storage includes a per-field visibility setting for each supported field.
When fetching another user’s profile, only fields marked public are returned (or otherwise exposed) to the requester.
When fetching the signed-in user’s own profile, all fields and all visibility settings are available.

requirements.text: Update the Profile & Settings UI to allow the signed-in user to edit bio and manage per-field public/private visibility toggles for all supported profile fields. All user-facing text for these controls must be in English.

quotes: lets kinda do all of those.
lets make those added too, maybe let the user decide also
Use English for any user-facing text in the build request.

acceptanceCriteria: Profile/Settings includes an editable Bio field.
Profile/Settings includes a visibility toggle per supported field (e.g., Display name, Avatar, Bio, Join date, etc.).
Toggling visibility changes what other users can see on public profile surfaces.
All labels, helper text, button text, and confirmations are in English.

requirements.text: Update all profile display surfaces (including the profile popover and any public profile UI) to show meaningful public profile information (e.g., display name, avatar, bio, join date when public) rather than showing only a Principal ID. Do not display non-public fields to other users.

quotes: all i see if i go to someones profile is their principal id, nothing else.
lets kinda do all of those.

acceptanceCriteria: Public-facing profile surfaces display public fields (e.g., display name and avatar) when available.
Bio and join date only render for other users if those fields are marked public by the profile owner.
No public profile surface renders a Principal ID.

requirements.text: Fix server management controls visibility so that server edit/delete (and related management) actions appear for the actual server owner. Ensure ownership checks are based on the server owner Principal and the frontend permission checks use the owner Principal returned by the backend.

quotes: and i cant manage servers or anything (dont see button)

acceptanceCriteria: If the signed-in user is the server owner, server management actions are visible and usable.
If the signed-in user is not the server owner, server management actions are not shown (or are disabled) as appropriate.
Ownership/permission logic is based on Principal identity, not display name.

requirements.text: Enhance @mention UX by formatting mentions in rendered post/message text and making mentions clickable to open a user profile surface (e.g., profile popover). Mentions must resolve to the correct user identity (Principal) for profile lookup, without exposing Principal IDs in the UI.

quotes: and acn you make the @mention do a little more?
maybe integrate in a notification center/popup place later.

acceptanceCriteria: Rendered message/post text visually distinguishes @mentions from surrounding text.
Clicking an @mention opens a profile surface for the mentioned user.
The UI does not reveal Principal IDs as part of mention rendering or interaction.

requirements.text: Add any required conditional state migration to preserve existing user profiles when upgrading the backend profile schema, initializing new fields and visibility settings with safe defaults and setting join date for existing users where appropriate.

quotes: ❌ Sorry, the AI couldn't implement that request. Please rephrase or simplify.

acceptanceCriteria: Upgrading an existing deployment does not break profile reads/writes for existing users.
Existing users have valid default visibility settings for all fields after upgrade.
Existing users have a join date value populated (or a safe/consistent fallback) after upgrade.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in backend/main.mo).
Only generate backend/migration.mo if required to preserve existing state during upgrade.
Do not edit any files under frontend/src/components/ui or other frontend immutable paths; compose existing UI components instead.
nonGoals: Do not implement a notification center in this build (only keep mention UX compatible with adding it later).
Do not add third-party authentication providers beyond Internet Identity.
Do not introduce real-time WebSocket updates; keep the existing polling approach.
